insights: Facsimile/printed signatures (60% of auto rejections) are distinct blocklist signal; only genuine on-card autos confirm as snipes.
Multi-card lots, incomplete sets, and stock photo listings (25% of rejections) fail despite matching player/card specs.
Wrong_market_price dominates rejections (40%); confirmed snipes show 80%+ delta vs. market avg, suggesting floor threshold ~$15+.
Parallel mismatches (Refractor listed as Base, wrong year variants) and wrong_player listings (15% of rejections) are caught by title/spec conflicts.
Reprint/custom (Rainbow Foil, Gold editions) and specialty parallels (Leaf Exhibits MSIG, Merrick Mint) systematically rejected.
Confirmed snipes are clean single-card, on-card signature autos (Authentic Images, Topps Tribute) with high delta and no title red flags.
Novelty card emoji titles and vague 'group of noted' listings indicate spam; clean, specific titles correlate with confirmation.
Ken Griffey Jr rookie parallels (1989 UD Base) rejected heavily due to market saturation; Ichiro/Ohtani autos more snipeable if genuine and priced <$20.
